name: Navigation
domain: Geodesics, optimal path planning, estimation, GPS and guidance systems
core_insight: The shortest path on a curved surface is a geodesic. Algorithms like Dijkstra find shortest paths in discrete graphs. The Kalman filter optimally estimates state by fusing noisy measurements with a dynamic model — the navigator's fundamental tool.

key_equations.name: Geodesic Equation
key_equations.why: Straightest path on curved space: Christoffel symbols encode how space bends the trajectory

key_equations.name: Kalman Filter
key_equations.why: Optimal linear estimator: innovation (measurement minus prediction) corrected by Kalman gain

key_equations.name: Dijkstra's Algorithm
key_equations.why: Greedy shortest-path on weighted graphs: relaxation guarantees optimal route

seed_prompt: ◆→ What is the curvature of the space? Geodesic first, then optimal estimate. Where does the path want to go?
